children.heading: .—Use of Retired Pay Base in Computing Retired Pay
children.label: a .—Use of Retired Pay Base in Computing Retired Pay
children.text: The retired pay or retainer pay of any person entitled to that pay who first became a member of a uniformed service after , is computed using the retired pay base or retainer pay base determined under this section.September 7, 1980

children.heading: .—High-Three Average
children.label: b .—High-Three Average
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (f), the retired pay base or retainer pay base of a person under this section is the person’s high-three average determined under subsection (c) or (d).

children.heading: Computation of High-Three Average for Members Entitled to Retired or Retainer Pay for Regular Service.—
children.label: c Computation of High-Three Average for Members Entitled to Retired or Retainer Pay for Regular Service.—
A
children.text: the total amount of monthly basic pay to which the member was entitled for the 36 months (whether or not consecutive) out of all the months of active service of the member for which the monthly basic pay to which the member was entitled was the highest, divided by

children.label: A
children.text: the total amount of basic pay to which the member was entitled during the period of the member’s active service, divided by

children.label: B
children.text: the number of months (including any fraction thereof) of the member’s active service.

children.heading: .—Special Rule for Reserve Component Members
children.label: 3 .—Special Rule for Reserve Component Members
children.text: In the case of a member of a reserve component who is entitled to retired pay under section 1201 or 1202 of this title, the member’s high-three average (notwithstanding paragraphs (1) and (2)) is computed in the same manner as prescribed in paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (d) for a member entitled to retired pay under section 1204 or 1205 of this title.

children.heading: Computation of High-Three Average for Members and Former Members Entitled to Retired Pay for Nonregular Service.—
children.label: d Computation of High-Three Average for Members and Former Members Entitled to Retired Pay for Nonregular Service.—
A
children.text: the total amount of monthly basic pay to which the member or former member was entitled during the member or former member’s high-36 months (or to which the member or former member would have been entitled if the member or former member had served on active duty during the entire period of the member or former member’s high-36 months), divided by

children.label: A
children.text: the total amount of monthly basic pay to which the member was entitled during the member’s high-36 months (or to which the member would have been entitled if the member had served on active duty during the entire period of the member’s high-36 months), divided by

children.label: A
children.text: the total amount of basic pay to which the member was entitled during the entire period the member was a member of a uniformed service before being so retired (or to which the member would have been entitled if the member had served on active duty during the entire period the member was a member of a uniformed service before being so retired), divided by

children.label: B
children.text: the number of months (including any fraction thereof) which the member was a member before being so retired.

children.heading: .—High-36 months
children.label: 4 .—High-36 months
children.text: The high-36 months of a member or former member whose retired pay is covered by paragraph (1) or (2) are the 36 months (whether or not consecutive) out of all the months before the member or former member became entitled to retired pay or, in the case of a member or former member entitled to retired pay by reason of an election under , before the member or former member completes the service required under such section 12741(a), for which the monthly basic pay to which the member or former member was entitled (or would have been entitled if serving on active duty during those months) was the highest. In the case of a former member, only months during which the former member was a member of a uniformed service may be used for purposes of the preceding sentence.section 12741(a) of this title

children.heading: 30 .—Limitation for Enlisted Members Retiring With Less ThanYears’ Service
children.label: e 30 .—Limitation for Enlisted Members Retiring With Less ThanYears’ Service
children.text: In the case of a member who is retired under section 7314 or 9314 of this title or who is transferred to the Fleet Reserve or Fleet Marine Corps Reserve under , the member’s high-36 average shall be computed using only rates of basic pay applicable to months of active duty of the member as an enlisted member.section 8330 of this title

children.heading: .—Exception for Enlisted Members Reduced in Grade and Officers Who Do Not Serve Satisfactorily in Highest Grade Held
children.label: f .—Exception for Enlisted Members Reduced in Grade and Officers Who Do Not Serve Satisfactorily in Highest Grade Held

children.heading: .—Computation based on pre-high-three rules
children.label: 1 .—Computation based on pre-high-three rules
children.text: In the case of a member or former member described in paragraph (2), the retired pay base or retainer pay base is determined under in the same manner as if the member or former member first became a member of a uniformed service before .section 1406 of this titleSeptember 8, 1980

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a member retired in an enlisted grade or transferred to the Fleet Reserve or Fleet Marine Corps Reserve, was at any time reduced in grade as the result of a court-martial sentence, nonjudicial punishment, or an administrative action, unless the member was subsequently promoted to a higher enlisted grade or appointed to a commissioned or warrant grade; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an officer, is retired in a grade lower than the highest grade in which served pursuant to section 1370 or 1370a of this title that the officer served on active duty satisfactorily in that grade.

children.heading: .—Special rule for enlisted members
children.label: 3 .—Special rule for enlisted members
children.text: In the case of a member who retires within three years after having been reduced in grade as described in paragraph (2)(A), who retires in an enlisted grade that is lower than the grade from which reduced, and who would be subject to paragraph (1) but for a subsequent promotion to a higher enlisted grade or a subsequent appointment to a warrant or commissioned grade, the rates of basic pay used in the computation of the member’s high-36 average for the period of the member’s service in a grade higher than the grade in which retired shall be the rates of pay that would apply if the member had been serving for that period in the grade in which retired.

children.heading: .—Special rule for officers who served in grade o–9 or o–10 and retired in grade o–8
children.label: 4 .—Special rule for officers who served in grade o–9 or o–10 and retired in grade o–8
A
children.text: the amount determined under subsection (c) or (d), as applicable; or

children.label: B
children.text: the amount determined under , as if the officer first became a member of a uniformed service before .section 1406 of this titleSeptember 8, 1980
